summary: Matched a broad slice of `ftCo_0A01.c`, raising the 81.08 KB file report by 7.79% and adding many C implementations for common fighter helper logic. The PR also exposed more of the `Fighter_x1A88_t` substructure, renamed `x61B_team` to `team`, promoted useful unknown prototypes to typed signatures, and cleaned assembly symbols/jump-table labels as decompiled code replaced asm.
what_changed: Implemented numerous previously stubbed `ftCo_0A01` functions, including helpers such as `ftCo_800A0508`, `ftCo_800A0CB0`, `ftCo_800A0F00`, `ftCo_800A0FB0`, `ftCo_800A1AB4`, `ftCo_800A1B38`, `ftCo_800A2170`, `ftCo_800A28D0`, `ftCo_800A2998`, `ftCo_800A3134`, `ftCo_800A5294`, `ftCo_800A5A28`, `ftCo_800A80E4`, `ftCo_800A8DE4`, `ftCo_800A963C`, `ftCo_800AA320`, `ftCo_800AABC8`, `ftCo_800AC30C`, and `ftCo_800AD42C`.
Renamed `ftCo_800A3180` to `ftCo_IsGrabbing` and `ftCo_800A3844` to `ftCo_IsAlly`, updating assembly references and call sites shown in `ftCo_0A01.s`.

Expanded `struct Fighter_x1A88_t` from an opaque byte array into a partially typed nested fighter substructure with pad inputs, command fields, fighter pointers, position vectors, bitfields, command-script buffer union, and half-width/half-height fields, guarded by `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Fighter_x1A88_t) == 0x57C)`.

Collapsed many former top-level fighter fields at offsets `1A88` through `1FDC` into `fp->x1A88.*` accesses, e.g. `x1A8C` became `x1A88.x4`, `x1A94` became `x1A88.xC`, `x1A98` became `x1A88.x10`, and `x1ACC` became `x1A88.x44`.
Renamed `Fighter::x61B_team` to `Fighter::team` and propagated the name through fighter initialization, collision, fighter library targeting, and Hassam item targeting logic.

Moved or exposed prototypes needed by the new C code, including `ftCo_80094150` in `ftCo_ItemGet.h`, typed `gm_8016B14C`, `grBigBlue_801EF844`, `grVenom_80206D10`, `Ground_801C4368`, `mpIsland_8005AB54`, and `mpLib_8004F008`.
Adjusted player CPU level/type storage and APIs: `StaticPlayer.cpu_level` and `cpu_type` became `u8`, while getter/setter APIs now take or return `int`.

Cleaned assembly labels by converting many exported `.sym lbl_*` labels to local `.L_*` labels and renamed jump-table data such as `ftCo_803C5B5C` and `ftCo_803C5C70` to `jtbl_803C5B5C` and `jtbl_803C5C70`.
Reduced symbol table noise in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, consistent with many labels becoming local or being absorbed by matched C.
smart_moves: The struct-first approach unlocked many matches: replacing raw `M2C_FIELD` and top-level `fp->x1A8*` fields with `fp->x1A88.*` made both `ftCo_0A01` and `ftCo_0B3E` code more readable while preserving offsets via a size assert.
Renaming behaviorally clear helpers to `ftCo_IsGrabbing` and `ftCo_IsAlly` improved call-site intent immediately and helped connect the matching code to gameplay concepts rather than raw addresses.

The patch used typed prototypes across neighboring systems instead of keeping `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`, which made cross-module calls match naturally and clarified return types such as `bool`, `s8`, and `float`.

Local inline helpers such as `inlineA0`, `inlineB0`, `inlineC0`, `inlineD0`, `inlineE0`, `inlineF0`, and `inlineF1` isolated repeated compiler-shaped expressions and condition clusters without over-naming uncertain behavior.
Assembly cleanup preserved jump-table relocation structure with `.rel` entries while converting labels to local `.L_*` names, keeping remaining asm searchable but less polluting globally.
The code retained explicit `PAD_STACK` and comments about fragile matches, e.g. the note that `ftCo_800A0F00` matches if `ft_80087A80` returns an `int`, documenting known matching constraints.
decomp_lessons: When a large fighter file is blocked by opaque offset fields, carving out a nested substructure with exact offsets can create a cascade of small 100% matches across multiple helpers.
Do not leave related APIs as `UNK_PARAMS` once call sites are known; typed prototypes for stage, item, player, map, and game-mode functions often fix both decomp readability and codegen.
If a field name has obvious global semantics, such as fighter team, rename it once and update all call sites rather than repeatedly reinterpreting numeric fields in individual functions.
For matching switch-heavy functions, keep jump tables explicit and named with `jtbl_` when still in asm; use local `.L_` labels for internal branch targets to avoid unnecessary global symbol churn.

Small return-type details matter: this PR changed `ft_80087A80` from `u8` to `s8`, player CPU APIs from `s8` to `int`, and `mpLib_8004F008`'s trailing parameter from `int,float` to a single `float`, all of which supported matching call sites.
Stack padding and volatile temporaries are acceptable evidence-preserving tools when they document compiler/codegen needs rather than obscuring logic.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_IsGrabbing` and `ftCo_IsAlly` use descriptive `Is*` boolean names for behaviorally identified common fighter predicates.
`Fighter::team` replaces `x61B_team` once the field meaning is known.
Jump-table objects use `jtbl_803C....` rather than generic `ftCo_803C....` names when the data is clearly a table of branch targets.
Uncertain nested fighter substructure fields remain offset-based, e.g. `Fighter_x1A88_t::x44`, `x554`, `x568`, while better-understood dimensions use semantic names `half_width` and `half_height`.
Local matching helper functions in `ftCo_0A01.c` were named mechanically (`inlineA0`, `inlineB0`, etc.), signaling they are codegen helpers rather than confirmed game concepts.
Map/island APIs gained argument names like `surface_idx`, and ground slope helpers gained `slope`/`intercept` parameter names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted many `.sym lbl_*` global labels in `ftCo_0A01.s` to local `.L_*` labels and adjusted branch targets accordingly.
Renamed asm function entries from address-only symbols to semantic symbols for matched predicates, e.g. `.fn ftCo_IsGrabbing` and `.fn ftCo_IsAlly`.
Preserved jump-table relocation arrays with `.rel ftCo_..., .L_...` entries while renaming table objects to `jtbl_*` where appropriate.

Used `PAD_STACK` in several functions to reproduce stack layout, including `ftCo_800A0F00`, `ftCo_800A0FB0`, `ftCo_800A1BA8`, `ftCo_800A20A0`, `ftCo_800A21FC`, `ftCo_800A5294`, `ftCo_800A8DE4`, `ftCo_800A963C`, `ftCo_800AC30C`, and others.
Used small static inline helpers to reproduce compiler expression sharing and clamp logic, especially for analog input normalization and predicate clusters.
Changed signatures and types rather than forcing casts where the decompiled call graph made the true type clearer.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The only explicit caution in the diff is an in-code `@todo` in `ftCo_800A0F00` noting that the function matches if `ft_80087A80` returns an `int`, plus header TODOs that static declarations belong in the corresponding `.c` files.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further name fields inside `Fighter_x1A88_t` beyond offset-style names.
Search for follow-up cleanup moving static declarations out of `ftCo_0A01.h` and `ftCo_0B3E.h` into their `.c` files.
Check subsequent PRs touching `ftCo_IsAlly` to confirm team-mode semantics around `gm_8016B168` and `gm_8016B14C`.
Look for additional matches in `ftCo_0A01.c` that reuse `inlineB0`-style analog normalization or `jtbl_*` switch-table tactics.
Search whether `mpLib_8004F008` signature remained stable after this PR.
